figures.caption: Figure 1: This schematic visualizes P ∈ P along with the induced P̃ and P̃0. P (X,Y, Z) is depicted as a continuum of 2×2 tables for binary X and Y . PX , PY and P (0, 1 | Z = z) are shorthands for PX|Z , PY |Z and PXY |Z(0, 1 | Z = z). A stratification S = {St : t ∈ [T ]} discretizes Z into Z̃ ∈ [T ] where the event {Z ∈ St} is represented as {Z̃ = 1}. As such, each cell probability of P̃ is P (X,Y | Z ∈ St) = EZtP (X,Y | Zt) where Zt follows PZ truncated to St. The CI projection factorizes the probability into the product of two marginals, which incurs an error captured by DTV (P̃ n, P̃n 0 ).
Figure 4: Results of Simulation 1. In the ROC plots, a 45◦ reference line through the origin is included as a gray dotted line.

Figure 2: A pictorial illustration of the DAG for a three-way table n(I3, J2,S) in Theorem 5. nS(i, j) is a shorthand for a two-way table n(Ii, Jj , S) at stratum S ∈ S. nS(i− 1, j) constitutes row sums, nS(i, j − 1) column sums of all the 2×2 tables of nS(i, j). For example, given the margin totals nS(3, 0) and nS(0, 2), the skyblue colored cell in nS(1, 1) is sampled conditioned on the cells marked in gray in nS(0, 1) and nS(1, 0). The process proceeds along the edges of the DAG to generate nS(3, 2). Repeating this process for all strata yields a draw of n(I3, J2,S) conditioned on n(I3, J0,S) and n(I0, J2,S).

Figure 3: Results of Simulation 1. Probabilities of rejection are truncated at 0.35, and the nominal level 0.05 is indicated as a gray dotted line. In the ECDF and median log CPU time plots, a 45◦ reference line through the origin is also drawn in gray dotted line.

Table 3: Significant windows when X is ETA (rescaled to (0, 1]), and Y ∈ {0, 1} is trip request (Y = 1 if a request is made). For each window I × J where I ∈ Il1 and J ∈ Il2 , αn(I, J) denotes the Šidákcorrected significance level, while p(I, J,SIJ) is the raw p-value prior to correction. θ̂S and σ̂(θ̂S) represent the estimated common log odds ratio for the window and its standard error, respectively. Confidence intervals are reported as θ̂S ± 1.96σ̂(θ̂S) following Agresti [2013, p. 230].

Figure 6: For each window (row), the empirical distributions of θ̂(S)—the stratum-specific sample log odds ratios—are plotted against the stratum-specific sample means of the conditioning variables on x-axes. Factors related to the fare components include time, distance, and base fare, among others. The common log odds ratio θ̂S of the window is indicated by the red line. For confidentiality reasons, x-axes tick marks and individual points are not displayed. Instead, fitted regression lines (blue) and their associated 95% confidence bands (sky-blue shading) illustrate how θ̂(S) varies with the conditioning variables. ρ denotes Pearson’s correlation coefficient.
Figure 5: Results from Simulation 2. The gray dotted line is a 45◦ reference. The results of each method are plotted only if they could be executed within the 64 GB RAM budget.
Table 1: Notations for contingency tables tabulated from {(Xi, Yi, Zi) : i ∈ [n]}.
